LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BARBARA MCKEE, ANDRE HAKKAK AND WHITE OAK GLOBAL ADVISORS, LLC'S MOTION TO SEAL THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE AND THE MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES FILED IN SUPPORT OF DEMURRERS IS GRANTED. THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE AND THE MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANTS DEMURRERS TO COMPLAINT OF KENNETH MASTERS AND THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE MADE IN CONNECTION WITH DEFENDANTS' DEMURRERS TO COMPLAINT OF KENNETH MASTERS, AND LODGED CONDITIONALLY UNDER SEAL ON MARCH 24, 2014, SHALL BE FILED UNDER SEAL. THE COURT FINDS THERE EXISTS AN OVERRIDING INTEREST THAT OVERCOMES THE RIGHT OF PUBLIC ACCESS TO THE RECORD (A CONFIDENTIAL SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IN ANOTHER MATTER); THE OVERRIDING INTEREST SUPPORTS SEALING THE RECORDS; A SUBSTANTIAL PROBABILITY EXISTS THAT THE OVERRIDING INTEREST WILL BE PREJUDICED IF THE RECORD IS NOT SEALED; THE PROPOSED SEALING IS NARROWLY TAILORED IN THAT ONLY THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT ITSELF (EXHIBIT A) AND REFERENCES TO IT IN THE MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES WILL BE SEALED; AND NO LESS RESTRICTIVE MEANS EXIST TO ACHIEVE THE OVERRIDING INTEREST. NO OPPOSITION WAS FILED TO THE MOTION. THE MOTION COMPLIES WITH RULE OF COURT 2.550 ET SEQ. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

PLAINTIFF KENNETH MASTERS SHALL SERVE FURTHER VERIFIED RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION NOS. 3 - 19 NO LATER THAN NOVEMBER 5, 2014. PLAINTIFF IS ALSO SANCTIONED FOR MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS AND FOR FAILING TO PROVIDE SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION FOR OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL. PLAINTIFF KENNETH MASTERS SHALL PAY DEFENDANT SANCTIONS OF $4,000 NO LATER THAN NOVEMBER 24, 2014. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. (302)

REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE ISO MOTION TO SEAL DOCKET BY KENNETH MASTERS (TRANSACTION ID # 58949459) FILED BY PLAINTIFF MASTERS, KENNETH AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF KENNETH MASTERS' MOTION TO SEAL THE ENTIRETY OF TWO CASE FILES IS DENIED FOR THREE REASONS. FIRST, THE MOTION DOES NOT ESTABLISH SUFFICIENT BASIS FOR THE SEALING OF ANY COURT RECORDS AS REQUIRED BY CRC 2.550. SECOND, EVEN IF THERE WAS SOME BASIS TO SEAL SOME COURT RECORDS, OR PORTIONS THEREOF, SEALING THE ENTIRETY OF THE FILES IS OVERBROAD. THIRD, SOME OF THE RECORDS SOUGHT TO BE SEALED HAVE BEEN PUBLIC FOR OVER TWO YEARS AND ALL OF THEM HAVE BEEN PUBLIC FOR MORE THAN ELEVEN MONTHS. NO EXPLANATION IS OFFERED FOR THE EXTREME DELAY IN SEEKING THE SEALING OF THESE RECORDS. THE MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO BEING REFILED UPON A MORE PARTICULAR SHOWING. MR. NEMECEK IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976.

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion By Kenneth Masters To Seal
Off Calendar May-25-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Nemecek, Jr. of Beugelmans, LLP telephone number 415-856-9174 for Plaintiff Kenneth Masters.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

Mr. Nemecek presented oral argument. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF KENNETH MASTERS' Motion To Seal the entirety of two case files is denied for three reasons. First, the motion does not establish sufficient basis for the sealing of any court records as required by CRC 2.550. Second, even if there was some basis to seal some court records, or portions thereof, sealing the entirety of the files is overbroad. Third, some of the records sought to be sealed have been public for over two years and all of them have been public for more than eleven months. No explanation is offered for the extreme delay in seeking the sealing of these records. The motion is denied without prejudice to being refiled upon a more particular showing. Mr. Nemecek is to prepare a form of order.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, To Compel Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanction Against Kenneth Masters In The Amount Of $8295
Off Calendar Oct-24-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. STEPHEN SOMMERS, TEL. NO. 524-2860 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MICHAEL J. BETZ OF ALLEN MATKINS LLP, TEL. NO. 273-7453 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WHITE OAK GLOBAL ADVISORS, LLC's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, To Compel Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanction Against Kenneth Masters In The Amount Of $8295 - Defendant's Motion is granted. Plaintiff Kenneth Masters shall serve further verified responses to Requests for Production Nos. 3 - 19 no later than November 5, 2014. Plaintiff is also sanctioned for misuse of the discovery process and for failing to provide substantial justification for opposition to Defendant's Motion to Compel. Plaintiff Kenneth Masters shall pay Defendant sanctions of $4,000 no later than November 24, 2014. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/ehg

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-07-2014 Continued To Sep-17-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-01-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ANDRE HAKKAK'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The First Amended Complaint ("FAC") does not state a cause of action for breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing against Defendants Barbara Jean Schassar McKee and Andre Hakkak because it does not allege that these Defendants had any specific contractual obligations. (Racine & Laramie, Ltd. v. Dept. of Parks and Recreation (1992) 11 Cal.App.4th 1026, 1031, "[t]he implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing rests upon the existence of some specific contractual obligation.") Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege facts establishing a contractual obligation, or to state a cause of action for intentional interference with contractual relations. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

Notice Of Motion To Seal The Request For Judicial Notice And The Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Filed In Support Of Demurrers
Off Calendar Apr-22-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANTS BARBARA MCKEE, ANDRE HAKKAK and WHITE OAK GLOBAL ADVISORS, LLC'S Motion To Seal The Request For Judicial Notice And The Memorandum Of Points And Authorities Filed In Support Of Demurrers is granted. The Request for Judicial Notice and the Memorandum of Points and Authorities in Support of Defendants Demurrers to Complaint of Kenneth Masters and the Request for Judicial Notice made in connection with Defendants' Demurrers to Complaint of Kenneth Masters, and lodged conditionally under seal on March 24, 2014, shall be filed under seal. The Court finds there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record (a confidential settlement agreement in another matter); the overriding interest supports sealing the records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored in that only the settlement agreement itself (Exhibit A) and references to it in the Memorandum of Points and Authorities will be sealed; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. No opposition was filed to the motion. The motion complies with Rule of Court 2.550 et seq. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.
